title: The Edinburgh Review, and Vestiges of the Natural History of Creation
summary: This 1845 article reviews Professor Adam Sedgwick’s incredibly harsh, Edinburgh Review critique of the anonymous, evolution-themed book Vestiges of the Natural History of Creation. While acknowledging the book's scientific flaws, the contributor defends its grand philosophical view of a divinely ordered universe and chides established geologists for intolerantly attacking an outsider's theories.
